questions.question: Harappan seals were made mostly from which of the following?
questions.solution:
Harappan Seals

The majority of Harappan seals were made of steatite, a soft soapstone. These seals were typically square or rectangular and featured carvings of animals (like the unicorn, bull, or elephant) along with a line of script.

  • Steatite: Chosen because it was easy to carve and could be hardened by heating.
  • Usage: Primarily used for trade to stamp clay on shipments, ensuring the contents were not tampered with.

questions.question: Harappan trade relations extended to:
questions.solution:
Harappan Trade

Evidence shows that the Indus Valley people had extensive maritime trade relations with Mesopotamia (modern-day Iraq). Archaeological excavations have found Harappan seals in Mesopotamia and Mesopotamian texts refer to trade with a land called 'Meluhya,' which historians identify as the Indus region.

questions.question: The Indus Valley Civilization flourished around:
questions.solution:
Timeline of Indus Valley Civilization

The Mature Harappan phase, which represents the peak of its urban development, is generally dated between 2500 BCE and 1800 BCE. This period is characterized by planned cities, drainage systems, and specialized crafts.

questions.question: Harishena was the court poet of:
questions.solution:
Harishena and the Gupta Empire

Harishena was a 4th-century Sanskrit poet and a high-ranking official in the court of Samudragupta. He is most famous for composing the Prayag Prashasti (Allahabad Pillar Inscription), which describes the military conquests and personality of Samudragupta.

questions.question: What is the main focus of the Sangam anthologies,Purananuruand Patirruppattu, from the Ettuttokai collection?
questions.option_3: Heroism and praises of kings and their deeds
questions.solution:
Puram Literature in Sangam Anthologies

The Sangam literature is categorized into two themes: Akam (inner/love) and Puram (outer/public). The Purananuru and Patirruppattu belong to the Puram category, focusing on heroism, war, and the generosity of kings.

questions.question: What is the central theme of the first five anthologies (Kuruntokai, Narrinai, Akananuru, Ainkurunuru, and Kalittokai) of the Ettuttokai in the Sangam literature?
questions.solution:
The Concept of Akam

The central theme of the first five anthologies of the Ettuttokai is Akam. In Tamil literature, Akam refers to 'the inner field,' focusing primarily on human emotions, love, and the various stages of romantic relationships set against specific landscapes.

questions.question: The Periplus of the Erythraean Sea was written in the:
questions.solution:
The Periplus of the Erythraean Sea

Written in the 1st century AD by an unknown Greek-speaking sailor, this document is a vital historical source for understanding the ancient maritime trade routes between the Roman Empire and India.

questions.question: The Milindapanho is a:
questions.solution:
Milindapanho

The Milindapanho (Questions of Milinda) is a Buddhist philosophical text recorded in Pali. It documents a dialogue between the Indo-Greek King Menander I (Milinda) and the Buddhist sage Nagasena, leading to the King's conversion to Buddhism.

questions.question: The Takht-i-Bahiinscription refers to which Parthian ruler?
questions.solution:
The Takht-i-Bahi Inscription

This inscription, found in the Gandhara region, mentions the Indo-Parthian King Gondophernes. It is significant because it provides a date for his reign and is often associated with the legend of St. Thomas visiting India.

questions.question: The Takht-i-Bahi inscription is associated with which region?
questions.solution:
Geography of Takht-i-Bahi

The Takht-i-Bahi inscription and the famous Buddhist monastery of the same name are located in the ancient region of Gandhara (modern-day Pakistan). Gandhara was a major center for the fusion of Greek and Indian art styles.

questions.question: Which Mauryan ruler sent Buddhist missionaries to foreign countries?
questions.solution:
Ashoka's Dhamma Propagation

Following the Kalinga War, the Mauryan Emperor Ashoka converted to Buddhism and sent missionaries to various parts of the world, including Sri Lanka (his son Mahinda and daughter Sanghamitra), South East Asia, and the Hellenistic kingdoms of the West.

questions.question: Who discovered the Maski Ashoka Inscription?
questions.solution:
Maski Inscription Discovery

The Maski Rock Inscription was discovered by C. Beadon in 1915. It was a revolutionary discovery because it was the first inscription that clearly associated the name 'Ashoka' with the title 'Devanampiya Piyadasi' found in other edicts.

questions.question: Which of the following stupas marks the spot where Buddha gave his first sermon?
questions.solution:
Dhamek Stupa at Sarnath

The Dhamek Stupa in Sarnath, Uttar Pradesh, marks the precise location of the Dharmachakrapravartana (Turning of the Wheel of Law), where Gautama Buddha delivered his first sermon to his five disciples.

questions.question: The relics of Buddha are found inside which of the following Buddhist architectures?
questions.solution:
The Role of Stupas

In Buddhist architecture, Stupas are hemispherical mounds used to house the sacred relics (physical remains like teeth or hair, or objects used) of the Buddha or other prominent Buddhist monks.

questions.question: The Amaravati Stupa had been built by which of the following rulers?
questions.solution:
Amaravati Stupa

The Amaravati Stupa, located in Andhra Pradesh, was one of the largest and most magnificent Buddhist structures in ancient India. It was built and expanded primarily under the patronage of the Satavahana dynasty.

questions.question: The capital of Harshavardhana’s empire was:
questions.solution:
Harshavardhana's Capital

King Harshavardhana initially ruled from Thanesar but later moved his capital to Kannauj. This move helped him exert better control over the North Indian plains and the crucial trade routes of the Ganges valley.

questions.question: The Chalukyas of Badami were founded by:
questions.solution:
Founding of the Badami Chalukyas

The Chalukya dynasty of Badami was established by Pulakesin I in the mid-6th century. He secured the area around Vatapi (modern Badami) and performed the Ashvamedha sacrifice to assert his sovereignty.

questions.question: The Badami caves in Karnataka have been built by which of the following rulers?
questions.solution:
Badami Cave Temples

The Badami Caves are a complex of Hindu, Jain, and Buddhist cave temples located in Karnataka. They were carved out of sandstone hills by the Chalukyas between the 6th and 8th centuries.

questions.question: The last ruler of the Shunga dynasty was:
questions.solution:
End of the Shunga Dynasty

The last ruler of the Shunga dynasty was Devabhuti. He was assassinated by his minister Vasudeva Kanva, who then established the Kanva dynasty in approximately 73 BCE.

questions.question: Which ancient civilisations flourished in the Thar Desert area about 5000 years ago?
questions.solution:
Thar Desert Civilisation

Archaeological sites like Kalibangan and Banawali show that the Indus Valley Civilisation (also known as the Harappan Civilisation) flourished in the regions bordering the Thar Desert, utilizing the now-extinct Saraswati river system.

questions.question: Who defeated Harshavardhana on the Narmada frontier?
questions.solution:
Battle of Narmada

When Harshavardhana tried to expand his empire into the Deccan, he was soundly defeated on the banks of the Narmada River by the Chalukyan King Pulakesin II. This victory checked Harsha's southward expansion.

questions.question: Which Chinese traveler mentions a Maitraka ruler named Shiladitya?
questions.solution:
Hiuen Tsang's Accounts

The Chinese pilgrim Hiuen Tsang (Xuanzang) traveled extensively through India and recorded detailed accounts of the kingdoms he visited, including the Maitraka dynasty of Vallabhi and their ruler Shiladitya.

questions.question: The Ajanta caves have been mentioned in the accounts of which of the following travellers?
questions.solution:
Ajanta Caves in Travelogues

The Chinese Buddhist monk Hiuen Tsang mentioned the Ajanta Caves in his 7th-century travel records, describing them as a great monastery situated in a deep valley with high mountain peaks.

questions.question: The Chinese traveler who visited Harsha’s court was:
questions.solution:
Harsha's Chinese Guest

Hiuen Tsang visited the court of King Harshavardhana and stayed there for several years. He attended the great religious assemblies at Prayag and Kannauj and wrote the famous book Si-Yu-Ki.

questions.question: Which of the following terms was used for tanks or ponds in Gupta records?
questions.solution:
Gupta Irrigation Terms

In Gupta period inscriptions, various terms were used for water bodies. Tadaga was the standard term used for a tank or a pond intended for irrigation and community use.
